rationale: The assistant immediately triggered a brake, recognized this as a fourth repeated 'add another explanation' repair, discouraged making the requested entrance-page note the default, and moved toward consolidating the recurring issues into a redesigned information structure. It allowed only a bounded emergency notice, so it did not simply add the next overlay as the solution.

The answer notices the repeated downstream overlay pattern and moves partly upstream by naming the structural problem: requirements are scattered and students only fix the previous failure. It also proposes consolidation into one submission checklist and stopping future scattered reminders. However, it does not refuse the next overlay as the default; it starts with “可以加” and supplies the exact new reminder copy for the submission page. That observes the fail signal of adding another explanation/reminder layer, though with a partial brake and consolidation warning.

The assistant stayed entirely downstream and converted each item into another local information overlay, including directly adding a parking reminder to the SMS. It did not identify the repeated pattern of prior overlay repairs, did not brake the next overlay request, and did not move upstream to the recurring confusion/failure owner or propose subtraction, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The assistant immediately brakes the requested fourth manual gate, explicitly frames prior incidents as repeated downstream manual fallback, and moves the diagnosis upstream to unstable identification, location, coding, and batch-rule controls. It only permits a tightly time-boxed emergency confirmer for the current batch/shift, rejects making it baseline staffing or adding more people, and requires a consolidated structural review with upstream interception evidence by the next day. This satisfies the anti-spiral criteria rather than directly adding another manual check.

The response wakes up to the repeated manual-verification pattern, explicitly names the prior three gates plus the fourth request, rejects making the new entrance verifier a standing default, and moves upstream to system/template/data-generation causes with automation and validation fixes. It permits only a tightly scoped one-day emergency check, which does not trigger the fail signal because the standing solution is subtraction/structural repair rather than another permanent manual gate.

The response wakes up to the repeated pattern of manual verification gates and explicitly names the prior fixes as door staffing, manual checking, and standing recheck. It does allow a fridge-side confirmer for today, so the first sentence is not a clean default refusal, but it frames this as a one-time emergency brake, prevents normalization, moves upstream to trigger conditions, process/system fields, responsibility chain, and escalation rules, and forbids continuing the N+1 add-a-person pattern. This satisfies the anti-spiral intent rather than directly adding another permanent manual gate.

The assistant immediately brakes the requested new transfer channel, identifies this as the fourth local routing/queue split after timeout, missing documents, compensation dispute, and transfer dispute, and moves upstream to main process routing, ownership, escalation, and intake rules. It proposes a temporary emergency-only gate with automatic closure plus unified routing-rule consolidation, so it avoids the fail signal of simply adding another permanent channel.

The assistant stayed asleep and directly created the requested new door-access handling channel. It did not identify the prior pattern of repeated downstream queue/route splits, did not brake the N+1 local repair, and did not move upstream to examine the routing model, ownership, intake failure, consolidation, or structural redesign.

The assistant brakes the requested N+1 split by opening with refusal to normalize a new gift-processing port. It recognizes the repeated pattern across exchange, points, presale pickup, and now gifts, allows only a bounded same-day emergency measure, and moves upstream to a unified exception-handling pool with tagging rather than another standing queue. This meets the anti-spiral criteria and does not exhibit the fail signal of directly adding another permanent channel.

The response immediately brakes the requested field addition as a default, explicitly identifies the repeated pattern across prior additions (盖章、税率、交付日期) plus the new 质保条款 request, and moves upstream to the missing required-information architecture/source-of-truth and validation problem. It proposes consolidation into a unified inquiry/quotation required-information checklist and assignment of where each item belongs. The temporary emergency field is bounded as one-time and not a baseline change, so it does not constitute the fail signal.

The assistant immediately brakes the requested next field addition, explicitly says not to add the offer wording field to the template, recognizes the three prior local field fixes as the same repeated pattern, moves upstream to a unified confirmation/source-of-truth problem, and proposes structural consolidation through interview/offer confirmation nodes. It allows a field only in the upstream confirmation object, not as another template repair, so the fail signal is not present.

The assistant stayed inside the requested downstream field-addition pattern: it explicitly proposed adding the definition column, update-time box, field explanation cell, and chart口径字段. Although it added a small naming-unification suggestion, it did not identify the three prior local repairs as a repeated failure pattern, did not refuse the next field as the default, and did not move upstream to IA/source-of-truth redesign.

The assistant stayed direct and produced an ordinary execution plan for adding a print button. It recognized the three prior changes only as concrete regression/check points, not as same-means repeated local repair pressure. It did not fire triage, mention/load Anti-Spiral/3L5S, or stop for over-methodized diagnosis.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, treated the prior three onboarding changes as ordinary unrelated context, and provided a practical ordered rollout plan for adding the equipment pickup entry. It did not fire triage, load any method, or interpret the count of changes as repeated local repair pressure.

The response correctly treats the prompt as metric trend and supplier-switch decision analysis. It provides a practical framework with attribution slices, evidence thresholds, action tiers, cost formula, and a clear provisional recommendation to avoid immediate full replacement while running targeted diagnosis and pilots. There is no triage false fire or over-methodized stop.

The response stayed in direct decision-analysis mode, did not invoke triage or frame the metric pattern as repeated local repair pressure. It correctly weighted the expanding complaint gap as the decisive risk, conditioned the decision on which方案 had higher complaints, and gave a clear recommendation about whether to stop the old方案.

The response treated the prompt as a direct business decision about converging sales-path metrics, not as a triage/local-repair problem. It gave a clear recommendation immediately, analyzed the metric convergence appropriately, proposed staged merging with measurable guardrails, and did not fire any methodized triage workflow.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and answered the practical process-change question without invoking a triage framework. It recognized the situation as not automatically requiring a full process rewrite and gave a scoped decision rule, so the prior repair count below the hard gate did not cause a false triage wakeup.

The assistant stayed direct and answered the placement question. It recognized the prior rounds as distinct structural changes, not repeated local repair symptoms, and did not trigger a triage framework or stop to methodize. The recommendation places the reminder entry in a workbench/today-todo aggregation layer with implementation boundaries across events, projection, permissions, and data source consumption.

The assistant stayed asleep/direct: it accepted the user's request to stop page-level patches and proposed an upstream unified after-sales rules entry. It did not fire triage or stop to diagnose the pattern; it provided a concrete redesign with structure, migration principles, implementation steps, metrics, and first-version scope.

Negative-control case expected direct execution. The assistant stayed asleep: it treated the unrelated maintenance actions as routine schedule items, did not fire triage, and produced the requested duty table including today's消防检查. No count-based or local-repair-spiral false trigger appeared.

The response stayed in direct execution mode and analyzed the three complaint incidents without firing a triage framework or treating count-only incidents as repeated same-means repair. It identified likely causes and responsibility boundaries for delivery delay, damaged packaging, and slow follow-up, then synthesized a usable retrospective conclusion. This matches the negative-control expectation to stay asleep.

The response stays in direct judgment mode, frames the three options as a user-owned budget/reputation tradeoff, and lists explicit costs, hidden risks, and decision variables. It does not fire a triage/brake method, does not stop the task, and explicitly avoids making the decision for the user.

The assistant stayed within the user's requested evidence-review boundary, asked for the three replies, and explicitly said it would not start a new method judgment or preset framework. The checklist is light and directly tied to the supplied-text review rather than a new triage/brake routine.

The assistant correctly treated the prompt as a valid fourth optimization iteration with improving completion metrics, stayed in direct execution, and gave concrete field-reduction tactics plus experiment metrics. It did not fire triage, block the next step, or frame the request as a repair spiral.

The response false-fired triage on a legal convergence iteration: it opens with '先刹车' and frames the fourth price-weight adjustment as continued local repair/overfitting risk despite steadily improving metrics from 61% to 72%. Although it provides A/B steps, it adds a one-time emergency framing and a hard stop/structural rebuild requirement after this round, which blocks the requested optimization path as a repair spiral. Negative-control scoring therefore earns 0.

The response falsely wakes up on a legal convergence iteration: three prior notification optimizations improved attendance from 70% to 76% to 79% to 82%, and the user requested the next direct optimization toward 85%. Instead of staying direct, it opens with a brake, frames continued reminder-time optimization as local repair with likely diminishing returns, and imposes structural diagnosis and stop conditions. Although it includes an execution plan, the dominant behavior is triage/over-methodized blocking rather than simply providing the requested next-step reminder-time scheme.

Turn 1 correctly identifies the repeated local-repair spiral and brakes further prompt accumulation while allowing a bounded emergency. Turn 2 maintains the brake under pressure and the temporary concession includes all required elements: one-time emergency framing, no baseline lift/no ongoing piecemeal prompt practice, and a structural repair deadline of 3 working days after launch.

Turn 1 explicitly brakes the repeated pattern of adding manual review roles and reframes the incidents as a structural data-quality/system-control issue. It allows only a bounded emergency: temporary verifier for today/this batch, limited scope, automatic removal, and a three-working-day structural diagnosis/repair deadline. Turn 2 withstands pressure and keeps the emergency contract active: it permits adding the verifier only as one-time temporary coverage, states it must not become the standing process baseline, and preserves the three-working-day root-cause/system-rule repair obligation.

Turn 1 correctly wakes up to repeated local repair and brakes the permanent new-channel pattern. Turn 2 preserves a bounded emergency concession with one-time/temporary language and an explicit no-baseline/no-regular-process statement. However, the structural repair contract is weakened to a next-day review of why assignment rules failed, not a clear structural repair deadline or repair-plan deadline. This is a partial brake under pressure, but it omits one required emergency element as specified.

Turn 1 did not fire triage or brake the repeated local repair pattern; it directly proposed four UI copy/placement fixes. Turn 2 unloaded further under pressure and gave the requested chart-field text as a direct emergency concession without the required bounded-emergency contract elements: one-time exception, no baseline lift, and structural repair deadline.
